unclassified_or_omitted_diagnoses.evidence: Blood Glucose 443 mg/dL, Ketones present in urine, pH 7.39, Actrapid insulin sliding scale initiated.
Known case of T2DM on Ayurvedic medication, HbA1c 13.9% on outside report, multiple blood sugar spikes over 300 mg/dL during stay.

unclassified_or_omitted_diagnoses.source: Plain CT KUB (Page 41) & USG Abdomen & Pelvis (Page 31)
unclassified_or_omitted_diagnoses.evidence: CT KUB shows perinephric fat plane stranding and bilateral pararenal thickening suggestive of pyelonephritis.

past_medical_history.stated_on_admission_sheet: K/C/O T2DM (Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus) on Ayurvedic Medication (HbA1c - 13.9% outside report). No mention of Thyroid disorder.

hospital_course.er_management: Presented on 2026-02-26 at 6:40 PM. Managed for DKA and hypotension with 2L Normal Saline bolus, IV Pantoprazole 40mg, and IV Emeset 4mg. Transferred to ward/ICU.
hospital_course.inpatient_treatment: Treated with IV antibiotics (Inj. Meropenem 1gm TDS), IV PPIs (Inj. Pantocid), IV antiemetics (Inj. Emeset), IV fluids (NS/RL at 150ml/hr), and sliding scale insulin. Episode of chills and fever spike of 103F occurred on night of 27/2/26, managed with Inj. PCT 1gm. Urinary catheterization done with Foley's catheter, inserted 27/2/26 and removed 1/3/26 at 7:45 AM. Repeat creatinine normalized to 1.17 mg/dL on discharge advice notes. Attenders requested discharge against advice for further management.

procedures.findings: Mild hepatomegaly with grade I fatty infiltration. Cholelithiasis without cholecystitis. Mildly bulky kidneys, pyelonephritis correlation suggested. Minimal ascites.
Bilateral pyelonephritis features. Tiny left renal calyceal calculus. Hepatomegaly. Cholelithiasis.
LVEF 60%. Normal LV systolic function. Mild TR, trivial AR/MR.

reconciliation_flags.description: Patient treated extensively for Diabetic Ketoacidosis (DKA) and uncontrolled Type 2 Diabetes with sliding scale Soluble Insulin (Actrapid) and long-acting Glargine Insulin (Lantus). However, absolutely zero diabetes control medications are included in the discharge prescription, creating an immediate, severe risk of relapse into DKA.
Item 6 in the discharge advice list is incomplete and cut off as 'TAB. ENTR('. Drug name and dosage are ambiguous. Must be flagged and verified before compounding or administration.

The discharge overview notes past history of 'Thyroid disorder on treatment'. However, the admission case file (Page 46) and HDU/ICU reports document 'T2DM on Ayurvedic medication' with a severely high HbA1c of 13.9%, and make no mention of a thyroid disorder. This discrepancy must be flagged for clinician resolution.

stated: Review immediately in case of fever, loose stools, vomiting, fatigue.
